• Another one: Kurtosis (Kurtosis) is the relative ratio of the mass of the distribution located in the center vs. in the tails. Kurtosis = 3 → Normal curve.  
• In this example, Kurtosis = 3.2, fairly normal, right?  
• No, there is a lot of central mass, but the histogram shows that the distribution is skewed and there are two outliers.
